characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Broken Bird: He was destroyed by Aidan's suicide. Becoming wrathful, manipulative and unreasonable with Jenny and going on the warpath for custody of Susie as she is Aidan's daughter. Despite Eva wanting to raise Susie herself and trying to comprise with Johnny by remorsefully telling him that she wants to make amends for what happened to Aidan as well as telling him that Aidan did know about his newfound parenthood. Cool Old Guy: Despite being in his early sixties, he can still hold his own in fights and was able to knock Phelan and Matthew to ground with a single punch. I Want My Beloved to Be Happy: When he found out about his Multiple Sclerosis. He chose to break up with Jenny so he wouldn't be a burden to her. They eventually patched things up and got married. After this when he thought he was going to prison he tried to make Jenny hate him to make it easier for her. Later he gave Jenny his blessing to date Ronnie Bailey as he thought Ronnie would make her happy even though she cheated on him with Ronnie. Jerkass Has a Point: He docks the wages of the Underworld Staff after discovering that they were avoiding work by watching a movie on working hours. Johnny wasn't wrong in punishing the workers and needed to display authority for their lack of respect towards him. Granted docking the wages was a bit extreme but a punishment was still needed. Papa Wolf: He punches Phelan for grappling Aidan. He also holds Tracey captive in the factory to prevent her from ruining Carla's wedding.

characters.name: The Rovers Return Inn
characters.description: Broken Bird: He was destroyed by Aidan's suicide. Becoming wrathful, manipulative and unreasonable with Jenny and going on the warpath for custody of Susie as she is Aidan's daughter. Despite Eva wanting to raise Susie herself and trying to comprise with Johnny by remorsefully telling him that she wants to make amends for what happened to Aidan as well as telling him that Aidan did know about his newfound parenthood. Cool Old Guy: Despite being in his early sixties, he can still hold his own in fights and was able to knock Phelan and Matthew to ground with a single punch. I Want My Beloved to Be Happy: When he found out about his Multiple Sclerosis. He chose to break up with Jenny so he wouldn't be a burden to her. They eventually patched things up and got married. After this when he thought he was going to prison he tried to make Jenny hate him to make it easier for her. Later he gave Jenny his blessing to date Ronnie Bailey as he thought Ronnie would make her happy even though she cheated on him with Ronnie. Jerkass Has a Point: He docks the wages of the Underworld Staff after discovering that they were avoiding work by watching a movie on working hours. Johnny wasn't wrong in punishing the workers and needed to display authority for their lack of respect towards him. Granted docking the wages was a bit extreme but a punishment was still needed. Papa Wolf: He punches Phelan for grappling Aidan. He also holds Tracey captive in the factory to prevent her from ruining Carla's wedding.

characters.name: 1 Coronation Street
characters.description: Arch-Enemy: Mike Baldwin, for literally decades, until his death a few years ago. Aw, Look! They Really Do Love Each Other: He and his father Frank often butted heads but he turned down a teaching job to look after Frank after his mother's death. Cultural Rebel: Ken was the first in his family to attend University and has always aspired to somewhat more intellectual than his neighbours and family. My God, What Have I Done?: Ken had this reaction when he was provoked into hitting one of his students in class. Not So Above It All: Ken likes to see himself as a morally upstanding man with a great moral compass but while he's not terrible by any means he also is more flawed than he likes to admit. He has a history of letting down his children, is a serial womanizer and often refuses to try to see things from the perspectives of others. This is often thrown in his face if he gets into arguements with members of his family.

characters.name: 2a Coronation Street (Above Audrey's Salon)
characters.description: The Ditz: Sweet but dim, that's our Maria. Extreme Doormat: Perhaps the reason why her relationship with Nick lasted as long as it did. Really Gets Around: It would be quicker to name the men in Weatherfield Mariahasn'tdated at some point. Her exes include Nick Tilsley, Tyrone Dobbs, John Arnley, Charlie Stubbs, Jason Grimshaw, Aidan Scott, Vikram Desai, Liam Connor... Dead Guy Junior: As said above, he was named for his dead father. Morality Pet: Tony Gordon had a soft spot for him and Gary does now.

characters.name: 3 Coronation Street
characters.description: Gossipy Hens: Rare male version. When someone is discussing something private in the Kabin or near him (or even not near him), he canalwaysbe seen trying to (non) subtly listen in. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Despite being a gossipy hen and trying to stick his nose into others' business generally, he does have his kind moments. For example, he comforted Rita when she was upset about the Rovers fire. Nosy Neighbor: To an extreme level. Will eavesdrop into others' conversations whenever he can. Small Name, Big Ego: Trust us when we say that Norris has an opinion oneverything! Camp Gay: In contrast to his ex-boyfriend Marcus Dent.

characters.name: 4 Coronation Street
characters.description: Jerkass: Treated Carla Connor like dirt after she was raped by Frank Foster and worked for him instead. Although she did apologise for it after, and is working with Underworld again. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: Sally may come off as unlikable at times but underneath her snobby behavior She is a deeply kind person. She loves her daughters,was the the only factory worker to acknowledge their fault in the factory roof collapse, not blaming it all on Carla, let Abi come to live with her and tried to expose Geoff when she knew it would strain her marriage with Tim. Jerkass Has a Point: She had no right to call social services on Seb's family, especially when they were making the effort to keep the family together with Anna's help. But, it's hard to argue with her when she said that the twins are safer because of it. Last Disrespects: She gatecrashed John Stapes funeral and loudly insulted him in front of his family and friends. In her defence John did kidnap Rosie prior to his death so she had a legitimate reason to spite him. Love Hungry: To Tim Metcalfe.

characters.name: 5 Coronation Street
characters.description: Fiery Redhead: Especially when Katy dumped him. Friend to All Living Things: Particularly to his dog Schmeichel. I Warned You: To Fiz, about John. Teen Pregnancy: He and his former partner, Katy. Abhorrent Admirer: Formerly to SteveMcDonald.

characters.name: 6 Coronation Street
characters.description: Apron Matron: Begins her time on the show quite driven and feisty. But Not Too Foreign: Was introduced as a member of the first Muslim family on the street, but this aspect of her character's been completely ignored over time, and she never wore a hijab to begin with. The Dog Bites Back: Eventually snaps, out of fear when Geoff corners her in the kitchen during one of his tirades, stabbing him with a broken bottle. Domestic Abuse: Geoff gradually subjects her to a heavy dose of the emotional variety. Extreme Doormat: Becomes this while married to Geoff, not leaving him after he locks her up for hours, repeatedly subjects her to horrific verbal abuse, forces her to clean obsessively and hires prostitutes (giving her an STI in the process) among other things.

characters.name: 7 Coronation Street
characters.description: Kavorka Man: Before his marriage to Sunita anyway. Arguably after, too. Large Ham: Due to his often lack of anindoor voiceandunusual speech patterns. Extreme Doormat: Asha was this in her relationship with Corey Brent, ignoring his flaws and allowing him to use her for sex. She finally had enough when Corey killed Seb though Unto Us a Son and Daughter Are Born: In 2006. Abhorrent Admirer: Norris certainly views her as this; he has never accepted her advances or her attempts to get close to him.

characters.name: 8 Coronation Street
characters.description: Arch-Enemy: Eileen Grimshaw, who took great pleasure in gloating when Lewis Archer stole all her money.It seems to have dissipated somewhat in 2016 after Michael (who both she and Eileen dated at different stages) died. Gail even asked Eileen to accompany her in the main car on the way to the funeral and they hugged after. It seems to have dissipated somewhat in 2016 after Michael (who both she and Eileen dated at different stages) died. Gail even asked Eileen to accompany her in the main car on the way to the funeral and they hugged after. Cartwright Curse: As she herself points out before Michael's funeral, most (four out of five; Martin Platt is still alive off-screen) of her husbands have died. Extreme Doormat: Her last husband was a drug addict who stole from her repeatedly and drowned while trying to fake his own death, but she loved him anyway.Her life savings were stolen from her by conman Lewis Archer. Her life savings were stolen from her by conman Lewis Archer.

characters.name: 9 Coronation Street
characters.description: Dogged Nice Guy: Even when Kirsty was beating him, he refused to fight back. Mr. Fixit: He is a car mechanic. Fiery Redhead: At times. Miscarriage of Justice: Was found guilty of murder, along with her husband John Stape, even though it was only him. Fortunately, he confessed before he died and she was released. Prison Episode: Several, when she was in prison before her trial for murder.

characters.name: 10 A Coronation Street (Above the front of The Kabin)
characters.description: Cool Old Lady: She frequently acts as a mentor to many a troubled young woman, such as TinaMcIntyre. Fiery Redhead: Though not a natural redhead,much to her embarrassment. Like a Daughter to Me: She sees both Jenny and Sharon as her own and always forgives them despite their track record of using her. Straight Man: To Norris's blusteringSmall Name, Big Ego. Heel–Face Turn: In the past, she acted as one of Callum's helpers and in fact helped torture David Platt at one point. Since she left her old neighbourhood, she's become kinder and even won a Weatherfield Good Samaritan award in 2016.

characters.name: 11 Coronation Street
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: Even by soap standards, Eileen has a hard life. Mama Bear: God help you if you hurt one of her boys. Disproportionate Retribution: Since his return, he concocts well thought out revenge schemes against those he feels have wronged him. Dropped A Bridg On Him: Following Bruno Langley's real life sacking, Todd suddenly left the show and went on the run off-screen after attacking a police officer. Gayngst: When he was going out with Sarah Platt, she was pregnant with their baby and he realised he was gay. It didn't help matters that he kissed her brother and was having an affair with nurse Karl Foster during it.

characters.name: 13 Coronation Street
characters.description: Good Parents: Stands up for his chidren always, and was very supportive of Jack during his sepsis storyline. Jerkass: To some since the affair, to others he's simplyStrangled by the Red String. Miles Gloriosus: Considering that he only ever starts fights with harmless characters. Mr. Fixit: He's a car mechanic, with Tyrone and Tommy. Papa Wolf: Mostly towards Rosie (even though the things he defended her from were usuallyherfault!)

characters.name: 15 A Coronation Street (Above the D&S Corner Shop)
characters.description: Cartwright Curse: Daniel's mum left him at fifteen for unspecified reasons, he was going to be a father but Ken convinced Sinead to abort the baby. When Sinead is pregnant a second time, she develops cervical cancer and has to choose between her life and the baby. Freudian Excuse: Denise abandoning him and Ken not bothering to keep in touch mkes it understandable why he was so messed up upon his 2016 return. Ken later causing Sinead to terminate his baby was a terrible thing for him to deal with and his anger was very justified though he handled the situation wrongly. Momma's Boy: Despite the fact that she left him at fifteen to live on his own. Parental Abandonment: His mother Denise left him to live on his own in their former flat when he wasfifteen. Prone to Tears: He is easily upset, especially if one says cruel about his mother.

characters.name: 15 A Victoria Street (Above Street Cars}
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: Nothing ever seems to go right for him. Extreme Doormat/Henpecked Husband: Witheverywomen he dates or is married to. Sad Clown: Steve showed signs of depression a few years ago but played it off with his usual humour. Mama Bear: She is the most motherly towards Amy and becomes the aggressive when she is in danger. This was shown when Carla's apartment was on fire and Liz shoved Tracey away so that Liz can convince Amy to climb down the ladder. Obnoxious In-Laws: She has never really liked any of her son Steve's wives, clashing particularly with Karen and Becky.Averted with Michelle, who she gets along well with.

characters.name: 16 A Victoria Street (Above Roy's Rolls)
characters.description: Benevolent Boss: It's a miracle he's still in business with how often he lets his workers take days off. Roy always treats his employees with the utmost respect. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: He might be a bit odd but he makes a bloody good full English breakfast. Cloud Cuckoo Lander: Regarded as one in-universe, though he doesn't fully deserve the reputation. Comforting the Widow: Cathy to him and him to Cathy. They both lost their spouses. Disappeared Dad: His father, St John Cropper, left him when he was very young and didn't try to contact him again until 2011. Unfortunately, the letter arrived too late (in 2013), and St John had died three months before.

characters.name: 19 A Victoria Street (Adjacent to the Builder's Yard)
8 Victoria Court
characters.description: All Girls Want Bad Boys: Her relationship with Aiden Critchley.Her interest in Callum Logan. Her interest in Callum Logan. Mama Bear: For Bethany and Harry. Misplaced Retribution: Her attack on Ryan Connor. Sanity Slippage: She had a guilt-induced mental breakdown after the murder of Callum Logan. She started to believe that he was still alive and out for revenge. She eventually got better after being committed to a psychiatric hospital.

characters.name: 9 Victoria Court
characters.description: Clingy Jealous Girl: Hated Steve's female classmate Andrea with a passion and gets very angry even if she merely appears in the Rovers to say hello. Jerkass: Her aforementioned irrational behaviour to Andrea.Her constantnagging, moaning, shouting and arm-crossingat Steve. Her constantnagging, moaning, shouting and arm-crossingat Steve. Took a Level in Kindness: Ever since finding out about Steve's depression, she treated him much better than she used to.

characters.name: 9 A Rosamund Street (Above Preston's Petals)
characters.description: The Alcoholic: Noteworthy on a show where most consume twice the British average amounts for alcohol and practically live in the pub. Peter is the only actual dry drunk, though by some measures most of the cast qualify as functional alcoholics. Arch-Enemy: Rob Donovan, his sister Tracy's former fiancé, and Nick Tilsley for a good stretch of time which peaked when he threw one of Sally's lawn gnomes at Nick's head. I Am Not My Father: Comparisons to dad Ken are something of aBerserk Buttonfor him. Big Brother Instinct: Towards Tracy, his "baby" sister. Kavorka Man: Downplayed. He's not tall or massively handsome conventionally but his easy-going charm is undeniable.

characters.name: 5 Grassmere Drive
characters.description: Settled for Gay: Her former partner, Gail's father, Ted Page, is gay. Verbal Tic: Has a tendency to say "Hmm, hmm!?" when angry with someone. You Have to Believe Me!: Audrey was the first to realise Richard Hillman was a murderer, but pretty much everyone thought she was just losing her marbles.

characters.name: 28 Grayling Street
characters.description: Dead Guy Junior: Tina was going to change his name to Joe, in honour of her deceased father before she gave him back to Gary and Izzy.

characters.name: Former Characters (A-Z)
characters.description: This is for characters who have either died or have left the street by moving elsewhere. Screw This, I'm Outta Here!: Gulati ended up leaving The Street in response to the loads of hatemail and racism she received from fans for her character's affair with Karl Munro. Took a Level in Jerkass Teen Pregnancy: She and her former boyfriend, Chesney. Hair-Trigger Temper: Flies into a rage at the slightest provocation, whether it's at Tim or Karl or Stella.
